volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 5.2.858 - Section 5.4.907)

sections.label: Section 5.2.858
sections.translation: What news are you bringing , I wonder ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.858b
sections.original: Nihil equidem nisi quod illum audivi dicere .
sections.translation: Why nothing but what I heard him mention .

sections.label: Section 5.2.859
sections.translation: What does he say then ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.859b
sections.original: Glycerium se scire civem esse Atticam .
sections.translation: That he knows Glycerium to be a citizen of Attica .

sections.label: Section 5.2.859c
sections.translation: Ho there !

sections.label: Section 5.2.860
sections.translation: Dromo , Dromo !

sections.label: Section 5.2.860b
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.860c
sections.translation: Dromo !

sections.label: Section 5.2.860d
sections.translation: Hear me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.860e
sections.translation: If you add a word — Dromo !

sections.label: Section 5.2.861
sections.translation: Hear me , pray .

sections.label: Section 5.2.861b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.861c
sections.original: Sublimem hunc intro rape , quantum potes .
sections.translation: Carry him off on your shoulders in-doors as fast as possible .

sections.label: Section 5.2.862
sections.translation: Whom ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.862b
sections.translation: Davus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.862c
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.862d
sections.translation: Because I choose . ( To . DROMO .) Carry him off , I say .

sections.label: Section 5.2.862e
sections.translation: What have I done ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.862f
sections.translation: Carry him off .

sections.label: Section 5.2.863
sections.original: Si quicquam invenies me mentitum , occidito .
sections.translation: If you find that I have told a lie in any one matter , then kill me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.863b
sections.translation: I ’ ll hear nothing . I ’ ll soon have you set in motion . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.2.864b
sections.translation: What ? Although this is the truth .

sections.label: Section 5.2.864c
sections.translation: In spite of it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.865
sections.original: Cura asservandum vinctum : atque audin ? quadrupedem constringito .
sections.translation: ( To DROMO .) Take care he ’ s kept well secured ; and , do you hear ? Tie him up hands and feet together . [*] Now then , be off ; upon my faith this very day , if I live , I ’ ll teach you what hazard there is in deceiving a master , and him in deceiving a father .

sections.label: Section 5.2.868b
sections.translation: Oh , don ’ t be so extremely vexed .

sections.label: Section 5.2.868c
sections.translation: O Chremes , the dutifulness of a son ! Do you not pity me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.870
sections.original: Tantum laborem capere ob talem filium ?
sections.translation: That I should endure so much trouble for such a son ! ( Goes to the door of GLYCERIUM ’ s house .) Come , Pamphilus , come out , Pamphilus ! have you any shame left ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.872
sections.original: Quis me vult ? perii : pater est .
sections.translation: Who is it that wants me ? ( Aside .) I ’ m undone ! it ’ s my father .

sections.label: Section 5.3.872b
sections.translation: What say you , of all men , the —?

sections.label: Section 5.3.872c
sections.translation: Oh ! rather speak about the matter itself , and forbear to use harsh language .

sections.label: Section 5.3.874
sections.original: Quasi quicquam in hunc iam gravius dici possiet .
sections.translation: As if any thing too severe could now be possibly said against him .

sections.label: Section 5.3.875
sections.translation: Pray , do you say that Glycerium is a citizen —

sections.label: Section 5.3.875b
sections.translation: So they say .

sections.label: Section 5.3.876
sections.translation: So they say ! Unparalleled assurance ! does he consider what he says ? Is he sorry for what he has done ? Does his countenance , pray , at all betray any marks of shame ? That he should be of mind so weak , as , without regard

sections.label: Section 5.3.880
sections.original: Morem atque legem et sui voluntatem patris
sections.translation: to the custom and the law [*] of his fellow-citizens , and the wish of his own father , to be anxious , in spite of every thing , to have her , to his own utter disgrace !

sections.label: Section 5.3.882
sections.translation: Miserable that I am !

sections.label: Section 5.3.882b
sections.original: Hem ! modone id demum sensti Pamphile ?
sections.translation: Ha ! have you at last found that out only just now , Pamphilus ? Long since did that expression , long since , when you made up your mind , that what you desired must be effected by you at any price ;

sections.label: Section 5.3.885
sections.original: Eodem die istuc verbum vere in te accidit .
sections.translation: from that very day did that expression aptly befit you . But yet why do I torment myself ? Why vex myself ? Why worry my old age with this madness ? Am I to suffer the punishment for his offenses ? Nay then , let him have her , good-by to him , let him pass his life with her .

sections.label: Section 5.3.889b
sections.translation: My father —

sections.label: Section 5.3.890
sections.original: Quid mi pater ? quasi tu huius indigeas patris .
sections.translation: How , my father ? As if you stood in any need of this father . Home , wife , and children , provided by you against the will of your father ! People suborned , too , to say that she is a citizen of this place ! You have gained your point .

sections.label: Section 5.3.893
sections.translation: Father , may I say a few words ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.893b
sections.translation: What can you say to me ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.894
sections.translation: But , Simo , do hear him .

sections.label: Section 5.3.894b
sections.translation: I , hear him ? Why should I hear him ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.895
sections.translation: Chremes ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.895b
sections.translation: Still , however , do allow him to speak .

sections.label: Section 5.3.895c
sections.translation: Well then , let him speak : I allow him .

sections.label: Section 5.3.896
sections.original: Ego me amare hanc fateor : si id peccare est , fateor id quoque .
sections.translation: I own that I love her ; if that is committing a fault , I own that also . To you , father , do I subject myself . Impose on me any injunction you please ; command me . Do you wish me to take a wife ? Do you wish me to give her up ? As well as I can I will endure it . This only I request of you , not to think that this old gentleman has been suborned by me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.900
sections.original: Sine me expurgem atque illum huc coram adducam .
sections.translation: Allow me to clear myself , and to bring him here before you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.900b
sections.translation: To bring him here ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.900c
sections.translation: Do allow me , father .

sections.label: Section 5.3.901
sections.translation: He asks what ’ s reasonable ; do give him leave .

sections.label: Section 5.3.901b
sections.translation: Allow me to obtain thus much of you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.901c
sections.translation: I allow it . I desire any thing , so long as I find , Chremes , that I have not been deceived by him .

sections.label: Section 5.3.903
sections.original: Pro peccato magno paulum supplici satis est patri .
sections.translation: For a great offense , a slight punishment ought to satisfy a father .

sections.label: Section 5.4.904
sections.original: Mitte orare : una harum quaevis causa me ut faciam monet ;
sections.translation: Forbear entreating . Of these , any one reason prompts me to do it ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.905
sections.original: Vel tu , vel quod verum est , vel quod ipsi cupio Glycerio .
sections.translation: either your own sake , or the fact that it is the truth , or that I wish well for Glycerium herself .

sections.label: Section 5.4.906
sections.original: Andrium ego Critonem video ? certe is est .
sections.translation: Do I see Crito of Andros ? Surely it is he .

sections.label: Section 5.4.906b
sections.translation: Greetings to you , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 5.4.907
sections.translation: How is it that , so contrary to your usage , you are at Athens ?
